name: Original Buffalo Wings
recipeInstructions:
1. Split the wings at the joint and remove the tips. Refrigerate in a single layer for at least 24 hours to allow moisture and blood to drain.
2. Preheat deep fryer to 350-360°F (175-182°C).
3. Fry wings in small batches until golden brown, about 8-10 minutes per batch.
4. Drain excess oil on paper towels, then immediately toss the wings in a large bowl with hot sauce and melted margarine.
5. Serve with celery sticks and blue cheese salad dressing.
Note: Adjust the amounts of hot sauce and margarine to personal taste preferences. For medium heat wings, use approximately 1 cup of hot sauce per 5 pounds of chicken wings and ½ cup of melted margarine. Some restaurants may omit the margarine entirely and vary the amount of hot sauce for desired flavor.
